summary: Add auth gate to Dad Joke of the Day app via requireAppLayoutAccess and registry update
implementation:
1. apps/web/lib/app-registry.ts: Change dad-joke-of-the-day entry from auth: false to auth: true.
2. apps/web/app/apps/dad-joke-of-the-day/layout.tsx: Import requireAppLayoutAccess from @/lib/require-app-layout-access. Make DadJokeLayout async. Add await requireAppLayoutAccess("dad-joke-of-the-day") as the first line of the function body.
3. apps/web/app/apps/dad-joke-of-the-day/__tests__/layout.test.tsx: Create test file following cringe-rizzler pattern. Mock requireAppLayoutAccess. Test four cases: (a) calls requireAppLayoutAccess with correct slug, (b) unauthenticated redirects to /login, (c) unauthorized redirects to /unauthorized, (d) authenticated user with permission renders children.
4. Run pnpm test to verify all tests pass.

testing.reason: Auth gating changes app behavior — unauthenticated/unauthorized users get redirected. Smoke tests verify the layout calls requireAppLayoutAccess and handles redirect errors correctly.

verification.reason: Auth gate is a server-side redirect — no visible UI changes. Behavior is fully covered by unit tests mocking requireAppLayoutAccess. Browser verification would require real auth state setup with no additional confidence over the tests.
